# WI-QUIC-CRT-0040 - QUIC client-side peer early-data disposition work item

## Summary

Add the peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition branch that surfaces accepted versus rejected disposition, discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection, and keeps public early-data support out of scope.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0143

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0038

## Planned Changes

- Observe the peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition and surface the narrow accepted-versus-rejected branch internally.
- Discard dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection while preserving accepted disposition for later traced ownership.
- Route malformed or duplicated `early_data` signaling through the existing fatal or update path rather than widening parser behavior.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for accepted disposition, rejected disposition, and malformed or duplicate handling.

## Out of Scope

- 0-RTT receive handling
- server admission
- anti-replay enforcement
- key update
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- public API widening
- public early-data admission
- IsSupported widening
- broader stream-management parity
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the early-data disposition branch

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for accepted and rejected disposition observation, malformed and duplicated early_data rejection, the randomized disposition boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the rejected-versus-accepted disposition cleanup branch; then run SpecTrace render and validation checks.

## Completion Notes

The managed client/runtime path now observes peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition, discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection, keeps accepted disposition intact, and leaves malformed or duplicated early_data data closed through the fatal/update path.
